commit.message:
Fix menu bar % and popover % out of sync

Root causes:
1. ModelType.from() didn't recognize "MiniMax-M*" (minimax-m prefix)
   or "abab" series models as .text, so primaryQuota fell through to
   first-in-array rather than preferring the LLM model.
2. primaryUsagePercentage was captured from whichever model the API
   returned first, while primaryQuota preferred .text models — the two
   could pick different models and show different percentages.
3. StatusBarController used primaryQuota?.usagePercentage (a separate
   code path) instead of usagePercentage (same value the popover shows).

Fixes:
- Add minimax-m and abab prefix to .text classification
- After building quotas, re-derive primary model using same preference
  logic as primaryQuota (.text first, then first), so all primary fields
  are consistent
- StatusBarController now reads usageData.usagePercentage (same source
  as the popover) instead of primaryQuota?.usagePercentage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Rust server streaming hang — return Working + delayed completion

The a2a-rs-server 1.0.18 SDK has a race condition where the streaming
handler broadcasts the initial task BEFORE subscribing to the event
channel. If the handler returns a terminal (Completed) task, the stream
hangs forever waiting for a terminal event it already missed.

Workaround: All streaming handlers now return a Working task and spawn
a background task that sends the Completed task via the broadcast channel
after 50ms. The SSE stream receives Working first, then picks up the
Completed event from the subscriber — no more hang.

Changes:
- SpecAgent now holds Arc<OnceLock<broadcast::Sender<StreamResponse>>>
- main.rs passes event sender from A2aServer to SpecAgent
- New send_delayed_completion() helper for async terminal events
- New tck_streaming_task() helper for all TCK stream handlers
- handle_streaming_sync() returns Working + schedules completion
- All 7 tck_stream_* methods use tck_streaming_task()
- supports_streaming() overridden to return true

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: reapply rotation transform on window/container resize

The .vr-wrap was created with fixed pixel dimensions captured at setup time.
When the browser window or player container resized, the wrap stayed fixed,
causing the rotation math (which reads container.offsetWidth/Height) to use
stale dimensions and render the video incorrectly.

Fix:
- Wrap now uses width:100% + CSS aspect-ratio so it scales with its parent
- A ResizeObserver on the wrap calls rotation.reapply() whenever its
  rendered size changes (window resize, player panel resize, etc.)
- Observer is disconnected in teardown to prevent memory leaks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent flaky ResourceSampling test from CPU measurement spikes (#192)

When SampleAllPluginsAsync is called back-to-back (as tests do),
wallClockMs between calls is near-zero, producing astronomically high
CPU percentages. This can trigger IsAggregateLimitExceeded(), suspending
the plugin before violations reach the warning threshold — causing
PluginResourceLimitEvent to never be published.

Changes:
- Initialize _lastProcessCpuTime and _lastSampleTime in constructor
  (not just ExecuteAsync) so the first sample has a proper baseline
- Add MinSampleIntervalMs (10ms) guard on CPU calculation to prevent
  unrealistic spikes from sub-millisecond elapsed times
- Lower test MaxMemoryMb from 16 to 1 for extra robustness

feat: add WebSocket live dashboard with transmission log and active transmitter tracking (#2)

* feat: add WebSocket live dashboard with transmission log and active transmitter tracking

- Add WebSocket hub for broadcasting real-time state updates to connected clients
- Track active transmitter (callsign + start time) with live on-air indicator
- Maintain ring buffer (50 entries) of completed transmissions with duration
- Wire reflector to push Notify() calls to web server on state changes
- Update dashboard template to show live client list, active TX, and TX log
- Add gorilla/websocket dependency

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* 🐛 Fix connection closure handling in WebSocket client
Ensure proper error handling when closing WebSocket connections in the read and write pumps.

* 🐛 fix(dashboard): HTML-escape '<1s' duration string

The '<' in '<1s' was interpolated raw into innerHTML, causing the
browser to parse it as an HTML tag. Escape it as '<1s' so it
renders correctly as plain text.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ✨ Add WebSocket origin allowlist configuration
- Introduced `AllowedOrigins` field in the config to specify trusted origins for WebSocket connections.
- Updated example configuration and README to reflect the new option and its usage.

* 🔥 Remove pull_request trigger from lint workflow

* 🐛 fix(reflector): reset watchdog start time on callsign change

tickWatchdog only reset watchdogStarted on idle → on-air transitions.
If a different callsign began transmitting before the watchdog expired,
watchdogCurrent was updated but watchdogStarted was not, causing the UI
start time and logged TX duration to be attributed to the wrong callsign.

Reset watchdogStarted and fire the notifier whenever the active callsign
changes mid-transmission, in addition to the existing idle → on-air path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ⚡ perf(ws): replace per-poll notify with event-driven + heartbeat

handlePoll was calling Notify() on every keepalive, generating O(nodes)
WebSocket broadcasts per poll cycle and growing unboundedly with node count.

Fix: only Notify() on new connections (structural state change). Add a
15-second heartbeat ticker in the web Server that periodically pushes the
current state so last_seen timestamps stay fresh enough for the UI to
derive Active/Idle badges client-side without a server push on every poll.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(runtime): close node-stop inbound ask race window (#936)

* runtime: fix two race-window bugs in hew_node_stop/node_inbound_router

Fix 1 – spawn-window close (node_inbound_router):
After INBOUND_ASK_ACTIVE is incremented, check shutdown_started
before spawning a worker thread.  If the flag is already set the
counter is decremented and the function returns without spawning,
so no new workers are created once node teardown has marked the
connection manager as stopping.

Fix 2 – per-conn_mgr drain (hew_node_stop):
Replacing the old global-INBOUND_ASK_ACTIVE drain (which caused
node_stop_wakes_pending_remote_ask to wait for workers on a different
node in multi-node tests) with a per-conn_mgr AtomicUsize.

Added field HewConnMgr.inbound_ask_active (Arc<AtomicUsize>) and
accessor hew_connmgr_inbound_ask_active().  InboundAskGuard is now a
tuple struct carrying an Arc clone of the per-manager counter; its
Drop impl decrements the global first, then the per-manager counter
(the drain watches per-manager, so global is always decremented before
per-manager reaches zero – no false-idle window).

hew_node_stop captures the Arc before hew_connmgr_free, then spins
(≤ 5 s) on the per-manager counter after the free returns.  Because
hew_connmgr_free joins the reader threads first, no new router calls
can happen after the free; the drain therefore terminates as soon as
all already-running workers exit.

Regression tests added:
* inbound_router_no_spawn_after_shutdown_started – marks conn_mgr
  stopping, calls the router directly, asserts neither the global nor
  the per-manager counter changes.
* node_stop_drains_inbound_ask_active – artificially inflates both
  counters, has a background thread decrement them after 60 ms, and
  asserts that hew_node_stop waited (elapsed ≥ 40 ms) for the drain.

Updated two existing guard unit tests (inbound_ask_guard_decrements_on_drop,
inbound_ask_guard_pair_decrements_twice) to use the new tuple-struct
signature.

* ci(nextest): add retry for circuit_breaker_trips_on_repeated_crashes

The test is timing-sensitive on Windows CI: it polls hew_supervisor_get_child
in the second crash iteration, but on a heavily-loaded runner the scheduler
can take longer than the 5-second window to process the supervisor restart
after crash #1.  When that happens the inner poll exits via timeout and crash
#2 is never injected, leaving the crash log at 1 entry instead of 2.

This is the same class of jitter already covered by the
connection_drop_wakes_pending_remote_ask retry.  One retry absorbs a single
transient hiccup without hiding real circuit-breaker regressions.

Observed: run 24272197851, job 70879288506 (Windows) — 12.09s, before=0 after=1.
Baseline on main: PASS 0.109s (run 24271597476, job 70877552018).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(overlay): derive initH from displayAR instead of wrap.offsetHeight

The wrap container (e.g. Bilibili player area) may have a different aspect
ratio from the video itself (extra chrome, letterboxing). Using wrap.offsetHeight
as initH caused:
  1. Black bars in the overlay on first open
  2. A sudden size jump on first resize because makeResizable enforces displayAR
     while the initial height was inconsistent

Fix: always compute initH = initW / displayAR so the overlay aspect ratio
matches the video's visual ratio from the moment it opens.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Per-channel FX routing via worklet isolation

Implements true per-channel effect isolation for libopenmpt playback using
secondary worklet instances. When a user routes an effect to specific channels:

1. A ChannelIsolationNode spawns a new AudioWorkletNode('libopenmpt-processor')
   loaded with the same module buffer, muted to only play the target channels
2. The main engine mutes those channels to avoid audio doubling
3. The isolated audio routes through the effect before joining masterInput
4. Transport events (seek, pause, resume, stop) are forwarded to isolation
   nodes to keep them in sync with the main engine

Architecture:
  Main worklet (ch1 muted) → separation → masterInput → [global FX] → output
  Isolation worklet (ch1 only) → BitCrusher → masterInput → [global FX] → output

Resource cost is minimal: libopenmpt renders in microseconds per 128-sample
chunk, and module files are typically <2MB. Each isolation instance uses ~2-4MB
of WASM heap.

New files:
- ChannelIsolationNode.ts: Wraps a secondary libopenmpt worklet instance
- ChannelRoutedEffects.ts: Rewritten to use isolation nodes instead of
  channelOutput tapping (which never worked for WASM engines)

Modified:
- LibopenmptEngine.ts: Added getModuleBuffer(), getAudioContext(),
  getCurrentPosition(), onTransportEvent callback
- ToneEngine.ts: rebuildMasterEffects now creates isolation worklets for
  channel-routed effects and updates the main engine's mute mask

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent flaky ResourceSampling test from CPU measurement spikes (#192)

When SampleAllPluginsAsync is called back-to-back (as tests do),
wallClockMs between calls is near-zero, producing astronomically high
CPU percentages. This can trigger IsAggregateLimitExceeded(), suspending
the plugin before violations reach the warning threshold — causing
PluginResourceLimitEvent to never be published.

Changes:
- Initialize _lastProcessCpuTime and _lastSampleTime in constructor
  (not just ExecuteAsync) so the first sample has a proper baseline
- Add MinSampleIntervalMs (10ms) guard on CPU calculation to prevent
  unrealistic spikes from sub-millisecond elapsed times
- Lower test MaxMemoryMb from 16 to 1 for extra robustness

commit.message:
fix(videoDetector): add maxWait to debounce to fix missing controls on first load

Pure trailing debounce resets the 300ms timer on every DOM mutation. On sites
like Bilibili, the player JS triggers hundreds of mutations over several seconds
on cold load, preventing findMain() from ever running. On refresh the page is
cached so mutations settle faster, making the bug intermittent.

Fix: track lastFire timestamp and force a check immediately if no check has
run in the past 1500ms, regardless of ongoing DOM activity.
